talk_title: Digital pathology models AEON and Paladin for tumor subtyping and molecular alteration inference

speaker.name: Kevin Boehm
speaker.affiliation: Computational Oncology, Radiation Oncology, Memorial Sloan Kettering Cancer Center

MOA_summary: AEON uses a knowledge graph–derived representation to infer 124 detailed tumor subtypes directly from H&E whole‑slide images, while Paladin conditions on each subtype to predict the presence of specific molecular alterations, demonstrating that digital pathology can capture phenotype‑genotype relationships for a subset of alterations (~4%).
key_findings: AEON achieved an average one‑vs‑rest AUROC of 0.996 across 124 tumor subtypes in the internal MSK test set.
Multimodal integration of AEON with the GDENS genomic classifier improved performance in both primary and metastatic validation cohorts.
Paladin correctly identified 9 of 11 ovarian granulosa cell tumors and outperformed DeepHistology on most molecular alteration predictions.
Only ~4% of examined molecular alterations showed a strong phenotype‑genotype signal on H&E, confirming limited but actionable predictive value.
Digital pathology aided variant‑of‑unknown‑significance annotation, low‑purity sample inference, and phenocopy detection.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: Most molecular alterations lacked a detectable histologic phenotype, limiting the utility of H&E‑based prediction for many targets.

combination_strategies.rationale: Combine histologic and genomic information to improve tumor subtype and alteration inference.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Higher AUROC and accuracy in external validation.

open_questions: Can allele‑specific effects of mutations be resolved by the current models?
How can the approach be extended to rare tumor subtypes with limited image data?

talk_title: Multi‑omic model for glioblastoma recurrence prediction centered on histopathology

models.name: Xenium (RNA) / Codex (protein)
models.genotype_or_key_features: single‑cell resolution spatial transcriptomics and proteomics

models.name: 19 GBM patients (total resection)
models.genotype_or_key_features: short, intermediate, long recurrence groups; 4 received PD‑1 inhibitor

MOA_summary: A pathology‑foundation model (UniHeal) is fine‑tuned to predict GBM recurrence time from H&E tiles, using MRI radiomics and clinical covariates as inputs, while spatial omics (Xenium, Codex) provide explainability by linking predictive tiles to gene‑program and immune‑cell signatures.
key_findings: Preliminary cohort of 19 GBM patients shows that short‑recurrence tumors exhibit disrupted tissue architecture and necrosis, whereas long‑recurrence tumors retain normal segmentation.
UniHeal embeddings cluster tiles into biologically meaningful groups (e.g., necrotic vs. vascular regions).
Spatial alignment of Xenium and Codex data reveals that longer recurrence is associated with enhanced immune activity, while short recurrence shows immunosuppressive signatures.
A K‑means spatial domain analysis identified an oligodendrocyte‑enriched niche (cluster 8) with increased immune engagement in long‑recurrence tumors.
open_questions: Will the model generalize to larger, multi‑institutional GBM cohorts?
How robust is the approach to varying adjuvant therapies (e.g., PD‑1 inhibition)?

talk_title: Unified single‑cell and spatial atlas of prostate cancer progression

models.name: Pan‑PCa atlas
models.genotype_or_key_features: 700k cells from 19 sites, epithelial, immune, stromal states

MOA_summary: Integration of >700 k single‑cell profiles across localized, metastatic, and neuroendocrine prostate cancer defines epithelial, immune, and stromal state trajectories, revealing SP1‑macrophage and mCAF‑centered immunosuppressive niches that drive progression and correlate with poor survival.
key_findings: SP1‑macrophage expansion is associated with lymph‑node and bone metastasis and predicts worse overall survival.
mCAF enrichment co‑localizes with SP1‑macrophage in a stromal niche (CN7) linked to poor outcomes.
Anti‑SP1 treatment in bone‑metastasis mouse models reduced tumor burden, pain, and prolonged survival.
NEPC emergence aligns with specific epithelial lineage‑positive states and distinct transcription factor programs.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: SP1‑macrophage mediates TGF‑β–driven immunosuppression, representing a selective vulnerability.

combination_strategies.rationale: Target the immunosuppressive macrophage niche to relieve TGF‑β–mediated suppression.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Reduced tumor burden and pain, extended survival in bone metastasis mouse model.

open_questions: Can SP1‑targeted therapy be safely combined with checkpoint inhibition?
What are the mechanisms underlying SP1‑macrophage recruitment to bone metastases?

talk_title: Deep learning profiling of regulatory somatic variants across clonal cancer evolution

models.name: SAIE framework
models.genotype_or_key_features: 22,000 chromatin profiles condensed into 40 sequence classes

MOA_summary: A sequence‑based deep learning model (SAIE) predicts the regulatory impact of somatic variants by comparing reference and mutant genome embeddings across 40 learned sequence classes, enabling patient‑level regulatory disruption scores that correlate with progression‑free interval independent of mutational burden.
key_findings: High regulatory disruption scores are associated with significantly worse progression‑free survival across TCGA pan‑cancer cohorts.
Severity of regulatory impact, not the number of regulatory variants, drives the prognostic signal.
Clonal versus subclonal disruption severity provides complementary prognostic information.
Six patient clusters based on disruption profiles show distinct pathway enrichments (e.g., immune vs. cell‑cycle).
open_questions: How do regulatory disruption scores integrate with coding‑mutation drivers for therapy selection?
Can the approach be adapted to rare pediatric cancers with limited variant data?

talk_title: Spatial multi‑omics integration to guide glioblastoma surgery and engineered macrophage therapy

speaker.name: Chibawanye Ene
speaker.affiliation: Department of Neurosurgery, MD Anderson Cancer Center

MOA_summary: Integrated spatial transcriptomics, proteomics, and mass‑spec imaging of glioblastoma core and edge identifies mesenchymal‑like and AC‑like cell states linked to TGF‑β–driven immunosuppression; engineered macrophages lacking TGF‑β receptors are designed to resist this suppression and improve therapeutic efficacy.
key_findings: Mesenchymal‑like cell states are enriched in hypoxic tumor cores, while AC‑like cells produce TGF‑β2 that activates tumor‑associated macrophages.
SP1‑macrophage and mCAF co‑localization defines an immunosuppressive niche associated with poor survival.
MasSpec Pen can detect metabolic signatures of proliferating (Ki‑67+) tumor cells intra‑operatively, potentially guiding resection margins.
CRISPR‑KO of TGF‑β receptors in engineered macrophages reduces tumor burden and pain in a glioblastoma bone‑metastasis mouse model.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: TGF‑β signaling from AC‑like cells creates a selective immunosuppressive pressure on macrophages.

combination_strategies.rationale: Render cell therapy resistant to tumor‑derived TGF‑β immunosuppression.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Enhanced tumor control and survival in mouse models.

open_questions: Will intra‑operative metabolomic signatures reliably predict residual aggressive cell states across patients?
How will engineered macrophages perform in the immunosuppressive brain microenvironment in humans?

talk_title: ADT Norm: non‑parametric batch correction for single‑cell proteomics across technologies

speaker.name: Ye Zhang
speaker.affiliation: Pharmacology and Computational Biology, MD Anderson Cancer Center

models.name: ADT Norm
models.genotype_or_key_features: non‑parametric peak alignment for ADT counts

MOA_summary: ADT Norm identifies negative and positive peaks in antibody‑derived tag (ADT) distributions using functional data analysis and aligns these landmarks across batches, removing technical variation while preserving biological cell‑type signals for downstream integration of CITE‑seq, CyTOF, and other single‑cell proteomics platforms.
key_findings: ADT Norm aligns background and signal peaks across heterogeneous CITE‑seq batches, achieving superior batch mixing and cell‑type separation compared with SeR normalization.
The method quantifies antibody staining quality, flagging markers with poor signal‑to‑background separation.
An AI‑agent interface enables natural‑language driven parameter tuning for large‑scale multi‑technology integration.
open_questions: How does ADT Norm perform on highly multiplexed spatial proteomics (e.g., CODEX) where signal bleed is prominent?
Can the AI‑agent be extended to automatically select optimal markers for downstream analyses?

talk_title: Foundation‑model‑driven MHC‑II peptide binding prediction with data‑efficient graph neural network

models.name: AlphaFold3 + ESM2 + GNN (trained on PepSeq)
models.genotype_or_key_features: structure embeddings + protein language model embeddings

MOA_summary: AlphaFold3 generates structural representations of peptide‑MHC‑II complexes, ESM2 provides sequence embeddings, and a graph neural network trained on low‑volume PepSeq binding data predicts peptide affinity, achieving state‑of‑the‑art performance with only ~2% of traditional training data.
readouts: AUC on IEDB CD4 epitope benchmark
key_findings: The GNN model trained on 2% of the data matches or exceeds NetMHC‑II Pan performance on DRB1 alleles.
Zero‑shot AlphaFold3 scoring (Fisher score) provides moderate predictive power, but the GNN substantially improves AUC.
Orientation errors in AlphaFold3 predictions for HLA‑DP alleles reduce performance, suggesting a need for orientation‑aware modeling.
Training NetMHC‑II Pan on the same limited dataset yields a 10% lower AUC, confirming the data efficiency of the new approach.
resistance_or_selectivity_notes: AlphaFold3 sometimes predicts reverse peptide orientation for HLA‑DP, leading to reduced binding prediction accuracy.

combination_strategies.rationale: Combine complementary predictions to improve overall AUC.
combination_strategies.phenotype: Slight performance boost over either model alone.

open_questions: Can orientation‑corrected AlphaFold3 models further improve predictions for HLA‑DP alleles?
How will the model perform on rare HLA alleles with minimal PepSeq data?
